channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Empowering women in tech: Lessons from a DWP Digital leader
item.description: I’m Head of User Support Services within DWP Digital. In simple terms, if your IT isn’t working, my team is your first protocol to fix it. I have about 450 colleagues in my function, and we support over 90,000 colleagues. I’m also Head of our IT service management profession.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 8 obstacles women still face when seeking a leadership role in IT
item.description: If you are a tech leader, you might encourage your daughter to follow your path, imagining a journey, like yours, with challenges that can be overcome with hard work. But if you are a man — especially a white man — you are likely unaware of the massive obstacles she will face that you didn’t.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: I was impressed by the Nacon Revolution X Unlimited Xbox controller at a recent event, and you can pre-order one right now
item.description: You can now pre-order the Nacon Revolution X Unlimited, an Xbox controller with Hall effect sticks and a handy LCD screen.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba says its new AI model rivals DeepSeeks’s R-1, OpenAI’s o1
item.description: Alibaba Cloud on Thursday launched QwQ-32B, a compact reasoning model built on its latest large language model (LLM), Qwen2.5-32b, one it says delivers performance comparable to other large cutting edge models, including Chinese rival DeepSeek and OpenAI’s o1, with only 32 billion parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI chatbots infected with Russian disinformation: Study
item.description: The world’s most popular artificial intelligence (AI) chatbots are infected with Russian disinformation, according to a study published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Breaking Speed Records with Micron's PCIe 6.x SSD Prototype
item.description: Micron's new PCIe 6.x SSD prototype shatters speed records, but real-world use is still years away
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City, rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate Democrat slams Trump's 'grift' meme coins
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) slammed President Trump for his launch of a meme coin in January, suggesting it might be the "biggest White House corruption in a century.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Trump administration just cut Defense Department grants that research terrorism and drug trafficking
item.description: Researchers in a highly regarded Department of Defense program called the Minerva Research Initiative recently received word that grants already awarded by the Defense Department are being terminated, potentially putting their work—and in some cases even paychecks for their students and lab staff—in
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Salesforce’s AI agents can decode tariffs in minutes
item.description: Tariffs have caused lots of headaches for business owners around the world, especially as they’ve become a hot topic in the political landscape. Just this week, President Donald Trump enacted 25% import taxes on Mexican and Canadian goods, causing the S&P 500 to plummet.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Seven important trends in the server sphere
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: With new division, AWS bets big on agentic AI automation
item.description: Amazon Web Services customers can expect to hear a lot more about agentic AI in future with the news that the company is setting up a dedicated unit to promote the technology on its platform. The division will be headed by Swami Sivasubramanian, until now Amazon’s VP of AI and data services.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: The Albanian Cabinet has decided to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: By Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls CHIPS Act ‘horrible,’ wants to defund it
item.description: In his address to Congress last night, President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act “horrible,” and said whatever money hasn’t been spent should now be used to reduce the national debt. The CHIPS Act, enacted into law in 2022 under then-President Joseph R. Biden Jr.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Intel creates a digital audit trail to trace the origins of chips
item.description: Intel is creating a digital audit trail in its chip supply chain for customers to trace how a chip traveled through the development process before reaching a PC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Expandable Touchdown: Revolutionizing Mobile Living Spaces
item.description: A Dutch company's innovative mobile living space, the Expandable Touchdown, is redefining travel and off-grid living experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PIX 11
